free_text: The (d,n) reaction on even argon isotopes.
The details of the experimental set-up-detector system.
For the Ar-40 reaction, natural argon served as target
material. In the Ar-40(d,n) measurement, the background
from the molybdenum foil was particularly disturbing.
Therefore at each angle an additional measurement with
the gas evacuated was performed.
For the reaction on Ar-36 and Ar-38 separated isotopes
were available, enriched to 99.9%. The Ar-38 gas
contained an admixture of 7% air. A special gas target
had to be constructed- it consisted of a 3.2 cm diam.
* 2 cm aluminium cup covered inside  tantalum foil.
The open end of the cup was closed with 3.4 mg/cm**2
molybdenum foil. The cell pressure was 56 Torr for
Ar-38 and about 100 Torr for the other two targets.
Some of the peak contents from Ar(d,n) could then be
obtained by correcting for contributions from the
contaminant reactions- on carbon, nitrogen and oxygen.
the 5.5 MeV Van de Graaff generator
The neutrons emitted in the reaction were
detected simultaneously by one detector at a fixed
angle for monitoring purposes and six moveable
detectors-NE213 liquid scintillators of 10.2 cm in
diameter and 2.5 cm thickness.
.The uncertainty of the absolute cross section
scale is about 15%.
.Digitizing error of angle.
.Relative errors are indicated by error bars.
figures.
.DWBA analysis.
Time-of-flight spectrum of neutrons was reported.
.By CAJAD.
